an5.28:1.1: “*Bhikkhus*, I will teach the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of noble five-factored right *samādhi*.
an5.28:1.2: Listen to that, attend carefully, I will speak.”
an5.28:10.1: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, on good ground at a four-way crossroads there stood a thoroughbred chariot, yoked, with the goad laid down.
an5.28:10.2: Then a skilled trainer, a charioteer of horses to be tamed, having mounted it, taking the reins in his left hand and the goad in his right hand, could drive it forward and back wherever he wished, in whatever way he wished.
an5.28:10.3: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, when noble five-factored right *samādhi* has been developed [*bhāvite*] in this way and cultivated in this way, whatever *dhamma* realizable by direct knowledge a *bhikkhu* inclines his mind [*cittaṁ*] toward for realization by direct knowledge,
an5.28:10.4: in each case he reaches the capacity to witness it, whenever there is a dimension [*āyatane*].
an5.28:11.1: If he wishes: ‘May I experience the various kinds of psychic power: having been one, may I become many … may I wield mastery with the body as far as the brahmā world,’
an5.28:11.2: in each case he reaches the capacity to witness it, whenever there is a dimension [*āyatane*].
an5.28:12.1: If he wishes: ‘With the purified divine element of hearing surpassing the human, may I hear both kinds of sounds — divine and human, those that are far and near,’
an5.28:12.2: in each case he reaches the capacity to witness it, whenever there is a dimension [*āyatane*].
an5.28:13.1: If he wishes: ‘Having encompassed with my mind [*cetasā*] the minds [*ceto*] of other beings, other persons, may I know them —
an5.28:13.17: may I know an unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*] as an unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*],’
an5.28:13.18: in each case he reaches the capacity to witness it, whenever there is a dimension [*āyatane*].
an5.28:13.2: may I know a mind [*cittaṁ*] with lust as a mind [*cittaṁ*] with lust,
an5.28:13.3: may I know a mind [*cittaṁ*] without lust as a mind [*cittaṁ*] without lust,
an5.28:14.1: If he wishes: ‘May I recollect my various past dwellings, that is: one birth, two births … thus, with details and particulars, may I recollect my various past dwellings,’
an5.28:14.2: in each case he reaches the capacity to witness it, whenever there is a dimension [*āyatane*].
an5.28:15.1: If he wishes: ‘With the divine eye, purified and surpassing the human … may I understand beings according to their *kamma*,’
an5.28:15.2: in each case he reaches the capacity to witness it, whenever there is a dimension [*āyatane*].
an5.28:16.1: If he wishes: ‘With the destruction of the *āsavas*, having realized for myself with direct knowledge the mind-liberation [*cetovimuttiṁ*] and liberation by discernment [*paññāvimuttiṁ*] that are without *āsavas*, may I enter and dwell in them in this very life,’
an5.28:16.2: in each case he reaches the capacity to witness it, whenever there is a dimension [*āyatane*].”
an5.28:2.1: “And what, *bhikkhus*, is the development [*bhāvanā*] of noble five-factored right *samādhi*?
an5.28:2.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, secluded [*vivicceva*] from sensual pleasures … a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*.
an5.28:2.3: He drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajena*]; there is no part of his whole body unpervaded by *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajena*].
an5.28:2.4: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, a skilled bathman or bathman’s apprentice, having poured bathing powder into a bronze bowl, would knead it while sprinkling it with water again and again. That ball of bathing powder would be suffused with moisture, pervaded by moisture, permeated within and without by moisture, yet not dripping.
an5.28:2.5: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajena*];
an5.28:2.6: there is no part of his whole body unpervaded by *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajena*].
an5.28:2.7: This, *bhikkhus*, is the first development [*bhāvanā*] of noble five-factored right *samādhi*.
an5.28:3.1: Again, *bhikkhus*, with the settling of thoughts [*vitakkavicārānaṁ*] and examinations [*vitakkavicārānaṁ*] … a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the second *jhāna*.
an5.28:3.2: He drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi*;
an5.28:3.3: there is no part of his whole body unpervaded by *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi*.
an5.28:3.4: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, there were a deep lake with spring water. It would have no inlet for water from the eastern direction, no inlet for water from the western direction, no inlet for water from the northern direction, no inlet for water from the southern direction, and from time to time the sky would not properly pour down a shower into it.
an5.28:3.5: But from that very lake a cool stream of water would well up and would drench, steep, fill, and pervade that very lake with cool water; there would be no part of that whole lake unpervaded by cool water.
an5.28:3.6: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi*;
an5.28:3.7: there is no part of his whole body unpervaded by *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi*.
an5.28:3.8: This, *bhikkhus*, is the second development [*bhāvanā*] of noble five-factored right *samādhi*.
an5.28:4.1: Again, *bhikkhus*, with the fading away of *pīti* … a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the third *jhāna*.
an5.28:4.2: He drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with *sukha* free of *pīti*;
an5.28:4.3: there is no part of his whole body unpervaded by *sukha* free of *pīti*.
an5.28:4.4: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, in a pond of blue lotuses, red lotuses, or white lotuses, there were some blue lotuses, red lotuses, or white lotuses born in the water, grown in the water, not rising above the water, nourished while immersed within.
an5.28:4.5: From their tips down to their roots they would be drenched, steeped, filled, and pervaded with cool water;
an5.28:4.6: there would be no part of all those blue lotuses, red lotuses, or white lotuses unpervaded by cool water.
an5.28:4.7: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with *sukha* free of *pīti*;
an5.28:4.8: there is no part of his whole body unpervaded by *sukha* free of *pīti*.
an5.28:4.9: This, *bhikkhus*, is the third development [*bhāvanā*] of noble five-factored right *samādhi*.
an5.28:5.1: Again, *bhikkhus*, with the abandoning of *sukha* … a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*.
an5.28:5.2: He sits pervading this very body with a pure and bright mind [*cetasā*];
an5.28:5.3: there is no part of his whole body unpervaded by a pure and bright mind [*cetasā*].
an5.28:5.4: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, a man were sitting wrapped from head to foot in a white cloth;
an5.28:5.5: there would be no part of his whole body unpervaded by the white cloth.
an5.28:5.6: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* sits pervading this very body with a pure and bright mind [*cetasā*];
an5.28:5.7: there is no part of his whole body unpervaded by a pure and bright mind [*cetasā*].
an5.28:5.8: This, *bhikkhus*, is the fourth development [*bhāvanā*] of noble five-factored right *samādhi*.
an5.28:6.1: Again, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*’s *nimitta* of review is well grasped, well attended to, well borne in mind, and well penetrated by discernment [*paññāya*].
an5.28:6.2: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, one person were to review another, or a person standing were to review one sitting, or a person sitting were to review one lying down.
an5.28:6.3: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*’s *nimitta* of review is well grasped, well attended to, well borne in mind, and well penetrated by discernment [*paññāya*].
an5.28:6.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is the fifth development [*bhāvanā*] of noble five-factored right *samādhi*.
an5.28:7.1: When noble five-factored right *samādhi* has been developed [*bhāvite*] in this way, *bhikkhus*, and cultivated in this way, whatever *dhamma* realizable by direct knowledge he inclines his mind [*cittaṁ*] toward for realization by direct knowledge, in each case he reaches the capacity to witness it, whenever there is a dimension [*āyatane*].
an5.28:8.1: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, there were a water jar set on a stand, full of water, filled to the brim, so that a crow could drink from it.
an5.28:8.2: If a strong man were to tip it in whatever direction, would water come out?”
an5.28:8.4: “In the same way, *bhikkhus*, when noble five-factored right *samādhi* has been developed [*bhāvite*] in this way and cultivated in this way, whatever *dhamma* realizable by direct knowledge a *bhikkhu* inclines his mind [*cittaṁ*] toward for realization by direct knowledge, in each case he reaches the capacity to witness it, whenever there is a dimension [*āyatane*].
an5.28:9.1: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, on level ground there were a square pond, embanked, full of water, filled to the brim, so that a crow could drink from it.
an5.28:9.2: If a strong man were to open the embankment in whatever direction, would water come out?”
an5.28:9.4: “In the same way, *bhikkhus*, when noble five-factored right *samādhi* has been developed [*bhāvite*] in this way and cultivated in this way, whatever *dhamma* realizable by direct knowledge … whenever there is a dimension [*āyatane*].
